behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real recursive filesystem watching (watcher config + OS event emission + daemon intake). MOCK-MISS: this is not a single-function rule; it appears at module/OS handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss platform watcher behavior for nested paths. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is purely filesystem processing with no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears for that nested file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is cross-module state handoff (request intake -> dedupe window store -> pipeline trigger), not necessarily real OS/network. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agent sequence, (d) no LLM-involved orchestration. Rejection gate: System is disqualified by text focusing on "processing request" + "time window" logic rather than watcher/OS event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake service + dedupe component + fake pipeline sink with a controllable clock; send two requests for the same path inside window and assert only first reaches pipeline, then advance clock past window and assert next request is accepted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could be Unit only if dedupe were a pure isolated function; this behavior explicitly targets request-to-pipeline interaction, so Integration remains the minimum realistic boundary.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission, so REAL-INFRA forces System. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher often emits idealized single events and can hide duplicate-event races/debouncing bugs. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by absence of AI/ML and multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher backend, perform a write pattern known to trigger duplicate events (e.g., temp-write+rename or rapid append+close), capture processing count, and assert exactly one processing for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure exists at OS watcher lifecycle boundary (delete/recreate inode/path, watcher rebind behavior). MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes rarely model real watcher invalidation/recovery semantics. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified because behavior text concerns filesystem resilience only, with no AI/ML decisions or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon on a real temp directory, remove watched directory while running, recreate it at same path, then create a new file and assert it is processed without daemon restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamp capture/precision under concurrent arrivals (OS metadata + intake ordering). Integration can test sort logic, but this behavior’s failure can come from real timestamp semantics and event timing. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by no AI/ML or agent pipeline; Unit disqualified because boundary token "arrive at the same time" implies concurrent infrastructure interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Using real filesystem, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled timestamp differences (and tie-case), run daemon, record actual processing order, and assert it matches creation-time ordering policy (including deterministic tie-break if specified).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If policy were purely in-memory sorting from supplied timestamps, Integration would suffice; this behavior text anchors on real arrival timing, so System is retained.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/locking/completion boundary (writer process behavior + watcher event timing + reader readiness). MOCK-HIDE: fake FS/events generally do not reproduce partial-write race conditions. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified since no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn a real writer that writes a large file in chunks with delays, trigger arrival event early, and assert daemon does not process until writer closes/completes; then verify exactly one successful post-completion transformation.
